name: Human contact layer — rebuild the network, reverse hermit mode
next_move: Populate people/ with actual entries beyond Rodrigo and Andre — old friends, past collaborators, people worth staying in orbit with. Each entry: name, role, last_contact, owed_response, relationship_weight, cadence (daily/weekly/monthly/dormant-but-keep-warm). Then build a Contacts view that surfaces who's aging out ('haven't talked to X in 6 weeks, weight 0.7, cadence says monthly — drifting'). Once that's live, the morning grid occasionally proposes a reconnection move scored by aging × weight × cadence.
blockers: Initial contact list — Jake needs to dump the names worth including
Cadence field schema: controlled vocab or freeform
live_band: Hermit mode is a specific cost of the AI work and needs a specific countermeasure. Declared as an anchor, it becomes a scoring dimension; undeclared, it stays drift. The grid should occasionally surface a reconnection move the same way it proposes a Rodrigo text. Medium-high weight (0.75) — important enough to score daily, not enough to outrank core-partnership fidelity when windows compete.

v1_scope.contacts_view: surfaces aging-out contacts with weight × cadence scoring

notes: Declared 2026-04-22 in Jake's scope riff. Comparator hint: relationship_network_fidelity.
